name: Mirador Morro de Toix Trail
name_original: Trail run al Monte Toix desde El Mascarat
description: This difficult 3.4-mile (5.4 km) jogging trail to Mirador Morro de Toix offers 801 feet (244 metres) of elevation gain in about 52 minutes.
region_intro: Jogging around Altea offers diverse terrain, from coastal paths along the Mediterranean to challenging mountain trails. The region is characterized by its prominent limestone ridges, such as Serra Gelada Natural Park, and the rugged Sierra de Bernia. Runners can explore routes along the Altea coastline, through natural parks, or beside the Algar River, providing varied experiences. This unique geography ensures a range of options for different fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Altea The most popular jogging route is Albir Lighthouse – Albir Lighthouse loop from Altea, a 7.7 miles (12.4 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 22 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers expansive views of the Mediterranean Sea and the Serra Gelada Natural Park. Another top favourite among local runners is Espigó Beach, Altea – Sant Pere Park - Altea loop from Altea, a moderate 5.7 miles (9.1 km) path. This route follows the Altea coastline, providing a flat surface ideal for a leisurely run with sea views. Local runners also love the Mirador Morro de Toix Trail, a 3.4 miles (5.4 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 52 minutes. Jogging around Altea is defined by coastal promenades, natural park trails, and routes with panoramic viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal runs to more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Altea are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 22 reviews. More than 2500 runners have used komoot to explore Altea's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Altea?
A: There are over 50 running routes around Altea, offering a wide variety of experiences for all fitness levels. You can find everything from easy coastal jogs to more challenging mountain trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging routes in Altea?
A: Altea's jogging routes offer diverse terrain. You can enjoy flat coastal paths along the Mediterranean, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21943656" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Espigó Beach, Altea – Sant Pere Park - Altea loop&lt;/a&gt;, or venture into more challenging mountainous areas like the Serra Gelada Natural Park and Sierra de Bernia. Trails along the Algar River provide a unique experience through lush riparian forests.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Altea?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 22 reviews. Runners often praise the stunning sea views, the diverse terrain that caters to different abilities, and the well-maintained paths, especially those found in natural parks and along the coastline.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running routes in Altea?
A: Yes, Altea offers several routes suitable for families. The coastal promenades, like the one featured in the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40383225" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beach Walk – Calle Bonavista Stairs, Altea loop&lt;/a&gt;, provide flat and easy surfaces with beautiful sea views. The path to Albir Lighthouse in Serra Gelada Natural Park is also popular and generally accessible for families, offering spectacular views.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Altea?
A: Many of Altea's running routes are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31507855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albir Lighthouse – Albir Lighthouse loop from Altea&lt;/a&gt;, which offers expansive views of the Mediterranean Sea and the Serra Gelada Natural Park.

Q: Can I find routes with significant elevation gain for a more challenging run?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a challenge, Altea's mountainous terrain provides routes with significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e872756397/mirador-morro-de-toix-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador Morro de Toix Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a notable example, leading through mountainous terrain with considerable ascent, offering excellent panoramic views.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see along the running routes?
A: Altea's routes are rich with natural beauty and landmarks. You can encounter the dramatic cliffside scenery of Serra Gelada Natural Park, the unique 'Forat de Bernia' natural tunnel in Sierra de Bernia, and the refreshing waterfalls along the Algar River. Coastal runs often feature stunning sea views and hidden coves. Notable highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/149038" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forat de Bèrnia&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/284114" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albir Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the jogging trails in Altea?
A: Yes, Altea is known for its scenic viewpoints. Many routes, especially those in the mountains or along the coast, offer unforgettable panoramas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e872756397/mirador-morro-de-toix-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador Morro de Toix Trail&lt;/a&gt; provides excellent views, and the Mirador de los Cronistas de España in the old town offers panoramic views of Altea Bay and the Serra Gelada mountain range.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by lakes or saltworks?
A: While Altea itself is coastal, some routes in the broader region can lead you to interesting natural features like saltworks. For instance, you might find viewpoints such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/889730" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Salinas de Calpe&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7344015" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas de Calp Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, where you can observe the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4371964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Saladar Saltworks&lt;/a&gt; and even flamingos.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Altea?
A: Altea offers pleasant jogging conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide mild temperatures, ideal for longer runs. Even in summer, early mornings or late afternoons are suitable, especially for coastal routes or trails along the Algar River which offer cooler, shaded sections. Winters are generally mild, making it a good destination for year-round running.

Q: Are there any routes that combine coastal views with a town experience?
A: Yes, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21943656" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Espigó Beach, Altea – Sant Pere Park - Altea loop from Altea&lt;/a&gt; follow the Altea coastline, offering stunning sea views and passing through parts of the town, allowing you to enjoy both the natural beauty and the local atmosphere.

Q: Can I find routes that are suitable for trail running?
A: Absolutely. The Serra Gelada Natural Park and the Sierra de Bernia mountain range provide excellent opportunities for trail running. These areas feature rugged terrain, varied elevations, and natural paths that are perfect for those looking for a more adventurous off-road experience.
tips: Rising to around 100 metres above sea level, a walk up the Mirador Monte Toix will give you breathtaking views. It’s an easy-to-moderate hike, just make sure you take your camera, because there are some spectacular scenes when you get to the top. Apparently you can even see Ibiza on a really clear day.

faqs.question: How long does it take to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: This jogging route is designed to be completed in approximately 52 minutes. However, your actual time may vary depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The Mirador Morro de Toix Trail is rated as difficult. It involves a significant elevation gain of over 240 meters, making it challenging for joggers, especially those new to trail running.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: While specific terrain details are not provided, given its 'difficult' rating and elevation changes, you can expect varied surfaces that may include uneven paths, rocky sections, and potentially steep ascents and descents. Appropriate trail running shoes are recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints or landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, a major highlight of this route is the View from Mirador Monte Toix, which offers stunning panoramic vistas.

faqs.question: Is the Mirador Morro de Toix Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many outdoor trails in the region are dog-friendly, specific regulations for this particular route are not available. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash and ensure they are capable of handling a difficult trail with significant elevation changes.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the Mediterranean climate, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for jogging. Summer can be very hot, especially midday, so early morning or late afternoon runs are advisable if visiting during those months. Winter can also be suitable, though conditions may be cooler.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from El Mascarat. You would typically look for parking options in or near this area to access the trailhead. Specific parking facilities are not detailed, so street parking or designated public parking areas would be the likely options.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that a permit or entrance fee is required to jog the Mirador Morro de Toix Trail. It is generally accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The Komoot tour data indicates this is a loop trail, starting and ending in the same general vicinity near El Mascarat.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a jogging trip on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given its difficult rating and duration, you should bring sufficient water, appropriate trail running shoes, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and possibly a light snack. A phone for navigation and emergencies is also recommended.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Due to its 'difficult' rating and significant elevation gain, this trail is generally not recommended for beginners or young children. It is better suited for experienced joggers or hikers looking for a challenging workout.
